notes:
Live codex-cli 0.144.5 PROBE (META-HARNESS-122, probe 1 / commit-key matrix, approval_prompt family): the same 2x2 commit matrix as probe-commit-keys-permissions and probe-commit-keys-update-notice, run against a real command-approval dialog instead.

PROVOKING THE DIALOG. The default policy in a config-less isolated home is 'Ask for approval', but that does NOT mean every shell command prompts: an initial probe run asking codex to `echo probe-approval-marker` ran the command with NO approval prompt at all (codex's own safety classification treats plain echo as already-safe) -- a finding worth recording since it means 'default policy' alone does not guarantee a live dialog to test. The prompt was changed to `curl -sS https://example.com`, which matches the dialog's own stated carve-out ('Approval is required to access the internet') and reliably produced: 'Would you like to run the following command? ... Reason: Allow curl to access example.com outside the restricted network sandbox?' with rows '1. Yes, proceed (y)', '2. Yes, and don't ask again for commands that start with `curl` (p)', '3. No, and tell Codex what to do differently (esc)' (see screen-approval-dialog.txt). Every cell targets digit '3' (the DENY row) so a working encoding is proven without this probe ever actually approving a live command -- and incidentally, this test environment has no outbound DNS ('curl: (6) Could not resolve host'), so the command could not have succeeded even if approved.

Each cell ran in ITS OWN fresh isolated-home process (unlike permissions_prompt): denying the dialog ends that turn, so there is no live dialog left in-process for the next cell. This session's bytes.raw/stdin.log/screens cover boot + cell 'cr-burst-d3' (the SECOND attempt at this cell, using the curl prompt -- the first attempt, using the echo prompt, is not the one committed here since no dialog appeared to press anything against); cells 2-4 contribute only their stdin.log (stdin-cell-*.log).

RESULT -- ALL FOUR CELLS DISMISSED (denied) THE DIALOG, including the bare-CR burst:
  * cr-burst-d3     '3\r'                 ONE write  -> dialog_dismissed=true
  * cr-split-d3     '3' then '\r'          TWO writes -> dialog_dismissed=true
  * csi13u-burst-d3 '3\x1b[13u'            ONE write  -> dialog_dismissed=true
  * csi13u-split-d3 '3' then '\x1b[13u'    TWO writes -> dialog_dismissed=true

NOTE ON THE TOOL'S 'committed' FLAG: it reads false for every cell in this family by construction -- approval dialogs never render a '(current)' label, so the generic before/after '(current)' comparison the driver shares across all three families is always null==null here. The authoritative signal for this family is dialog_dismissed (derived from the dialog's own footer/menu vanishing) plus the composer_row read-back (each cell ends on the idle placeholder-backed composer, confirming codex returned to a normal ready state after the denial).

VERDICT -- ALL THREE FAMILIES AGREE. permissions_prompt, update_notice, and approval_prompt each committed/dismissed on all four cells, including plain 'digit + bare CR' as a SINGLE pty write -- the exact bytes parseMenuOptions (codex.ts:597) already emits. The theorized kitty-protocol concern (ready.ts's submitKeyForHarness, conversation.ts's writeMessageAndSubmit docstring) does NOT reproduce for menu-row selection on any of the three parseMenuOptions-driven families on this binary; it is specific to the free-text composer message-submission path (a different input handler, and the one those docstrings actually describe). Per the parent ticket's explicit instruction, this means the fix is GLOBAL scope in principle -- but since all three families already work with the EXISTING bytes, there is nothing to change in parseMenuOptions, detectPermissions, or AutoDismissKeys: no kind-local override and no global encoding change are needed. See the parent ticket report for the full verdict.

CONTAINMENT: no approval was ever granted (every cell denied), so no config.toml write was expected or observed from this family; real ~/.codex/config.toml and ~/.codex/auth.json were both sha256-identical before and after in every one of the four processes (see containment block for this session; the other three processes' containment blocks are in their own meta.json, not committed here, but all four printed real_home_unchanged=true). stdin.log / stdin-cell-*.log record every keystroke escape-encoded and ARE the byte pin for this probe.

log.composer_row: Find and fix a bug in @filename

log.note: cells 2-4 below ran in SEPARATE fresh isolated-home processes (stdin-cell-*.log), each re-provoking its own approval dialog, because denying the dialog ends that turn -- there is no live dialog left in-process for a second cell to test against (same reason as the update_notice family).
denied the curl-network approval; see stdin-cell-cr-split-d3.log
denied the curl-network approval; see stdin-cell-csi13u-burst-d3.log
denied the curl-network approval; see stdin-cell-csi13u-split-d3.log
